meaning: adj.可实现的,可实行的,可了解的
examples: In many cases this realizable wealth is not realized during the lifetime of the home owner.
In an email, Savage told me, "I have a pro-realizable-fantasy bias."
That is why thought reform is a considered a desirable and realizable goal.
When mentioning tools the ideas stay down to earth, are realitic and maybe even realizable.
To listen to Mr. Kurzweil or read his several books (the latest: "How to Create a Mind") is to be flummoxed by a series of forecasts that hardly seem realizable in the next 40 years.
